name: South Korea
forecastReasoning: Highest robot density globally. Samsung and Hyundai both accelerating humanoid programs. Government 2030 strategy confirms policy tailwind.

name: Singapore
forecastReasoning: Top automation index globally. High wages drive strong ROI. Healthcare pilot signals service sector expansion beyond manufacturing.

name: Germany
forecastReasoning: Strong automotive manufacturing base. IG Metall acknowledgment of 2M jobs at risk confirms labor-cost pressure. High energy costs partially offset by renewable transition.

name: Japan
forecastReasoning: Pioneer industrial robotics ecosystem. Aging workforce creates strong demographic pull. Solar-powered agricultural humanoid signals service sector expansion.

name: Sweden
forecastReasoning: Very high wages accelerate ROI. Cheap hydroelectric power gives robot TCO advantage. Strong tech readiness index.

name: Denmark
forecastReasoning: Highest minimum wage globally creates strongest ROI case. Manufacturing share moderate but growing pharmaceutical automation.

name: Belgium
forecastReasoning: High wages and EU automation framework drive adoption. Chemical and pharma sectors leading.

name: United States
forecastReasoning: Tesla 1000-unit deployment signals industrial tipping point. VC investment in Figure AI confirms investor conviction. Manufacturing share low but logistics sector enormous.

name: China
forecastReasoning: 50,000-unit deployment largest globally. State-backed manufacturing and extremely low energy costs create massive adoption advantage. Manufacturing GDP share second only to KOR.

name: Taiwan
forecastReasoning: Highest manufacturing share globally. Semiconductor and electronics factories already highly automated. TSMC investments signal further adoption.

name: Czech Republic
forecastReasoning: High manufacturing share and EU single market drive adoption. Automotive supply chain integration with German OEMs accelerates timeline.

name: Slovakia
forecastReasoning: Highest manufacturing share in EU. Automotive-heavy economy with Kia, VW, Peugeot plants. Low wages reduce ROI urgency but volume makes aggregate impact large.

name: Hungary
forecastReasoning: Strong automotive base (BMW, Mercedes, Audi). EU regulatory environment and supply chain integration accelerate adoption despite lower wages.

name: Austria
forecastReasoning: High wages and sophisticated manufacturing ecosystem. Strong renewable energy profile reduces robot TCO. KUKA and AVL presence signals automation leadership.

name: Finland
forecastReasoning: Very cheap energy (nuclear/hydro) gives robot TCO edge. High wages and tech readiness. Nokia robotics R&D presence.

name: Brazil
forecastReasoning: Growing agricultural automation sector. Strong manufacturing base in São Paulo. Low robot density but high potential for leapfrogging.

name: South Africa
forecastReasoning: Mining sector automation accelerating. Strongest industrial base in Africa. Energy challenges create demand for efficient synthetic labor.

name: Nigeria
forecastReasoning: Nascent robotics sector. High mobile/tech adoption suggests future AI-labor integration. Low labor costs currently delay hardware ROI.

name: Argentina
forecastReasoning: Strong software and AI talent pool. Agricultural robotics adoption growing. Economic volatility drives interest in predictable automation.

name: Egypt
forecastReasoning: Strategic manufacturing hub for EMEA. Very low energy costs favor robot operations. Infrastructure projects incorporating automation.

name: India
forecastReasoning: Massive manufacturing scaling under 'Make in India'. Global capability centers (GCCs) shifting to AI agents. High volume makes even low % displacement significant.

name: Australia
forecastReasoning: World leader in mining automation. High labor costs create extreme ROI for humanoid deployment in remote areas. High tech readiness.

name: Chile
forecastReasoning: Copper mining automation drive. OECD membership accelerates tech adoption. Stable regulatory environment for AI investment.

name: Colombia
forecastReasoning: Growing tech hub in Medellín. Industrial diversification and focus on '4th Industrial Revolution' technologies.

name: Morocco
forecastReasoning: Major automotive manufacturing hub for Europe (Renault/Stellantis). Strategic position in green hydrogen could lower robot TCO.

name: Mexico
forecastReasoning: Nearshoring hub for North America. Automotive and aerospace sectors driving significant industrial robot adoption. Integration with US tech ecosystem.

name: Canada
forecastReasoning: High wages and aging workforce drive automation. Strong AI research ecosystem (Vector Institute). Resource sector leading in remote robotics.

name: Kenya
forecastReasoning: Silicon Savannah tech hub. Mobile-first AI adoption. Agricultural automation potential in export sectors.

name: Ethiopia
forecastReasoning: Emerging manufacturing sector. Large infrastructure projects. Early stages of tech integration in textile industries.
